semaglutide.black_box_summary: Thyroid C-cell tumors observed in rodents; contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2
semaglutide.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist — stimulates glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppresses glucagon, slows gastric emptying, and reduces appetite via central mechanisms
semaglutide.biosimilar_note: Peptide biologic — biosimilars in early development; core patents challenged
semaglutide.revenue_note: ~$14B+ annual revenue across all formulations (2024); largest revenue drug for Novo Nordisk

tirzepatide.black_box_summary: Thyroid C-cell tumors observed in rodents; contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2
tirzepatide.mechanism: Dual agonist at GIP (glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide) and GLP-1 receptors — reduces blood glucose, body weight, and appetite through complementary incretin pathways
tirzepatide.biosimilar_note: Novel dual-agonist peptide — more complex to replicate than single GLP-1 agonists
tirzepatide.revenue_note: ~$6B+ and growing rapidly (2024); fastest-growing drug in Eli Lilly portfolio

retatrutide.mechanism: Triple agonist at GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptors — adds glucagon-mediated energy expenditure increase on top of dual incretin activity; shows >20% body weight reduction in Phase 2
retatrutide.biosimilar_note: Not yet approved — Phase 3 in progress
retatrutide.revenue_note: Phase 3 — not yet approved or generating revenue

liraglutide.black_box_summary: Thyroid C-cell tumors observed in rodents; contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2
liraglutide.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist — daily subcutaneous injection; first GLP-1 RA to show CV mortality benefit (LEADER trial)
liraglutide.biosimilar_note: Core patent expired 2023; biosimilar entries expected imminently, already approved in some markets
liraglutide.revenue_note: Declining — peak ~$3B, now displaced by semaglutide within Novo Nordisk portfolio

dulaglutide.black_box_summary: Thyroid C-cell tumors observed in rodents; contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2
dulaglutide.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist — weekly injection using GLP-1 fused to Fc antibody fragment for extended half-life
dulaglutide.biosimilar_note: Patent expiry ~2027; losing share rapidly to tirzepatide within Eli Lilly's own portfolio
dulaglutide.revenue_note: Declining — peak ~$5.5B (2022), now falling as tirzepatide displaces it

empagliflozin.company_note: Co-marketed with Eli Lilly (our tracked NYSE partner); Boehringer Ingelheim is private
empagliflozin.mechanism: Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitor — blocks glucose reabsorption in proximal renal tubule, promoting glycosuria; also reduces cardiac preload/afterload via osmotic diuresis
empagliflozin.biosimilar_note: Small molecule — generic competition expected post-patent expiry ~2025; Eli Lilly will lose co-promotion revenue
empagliflozin.revenue_note: ~$8B+ annual revenue (2024); largest SGLT2 inhibitor globally

dapagliflozin.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitor — first SGLT2i approved for heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) regardless of T2D status (DAPA-HF trial)
dapagliflozin.biosimilar_note: Small molecule — generic entries expected post-patent expiry
dapagliflozin.revenue_note: ~$3.5B annual revenue (2024); important growth driver for AstraZeneca

canagliflozin.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitor — first approved in class (2013); also inhibits SGLT1 at higher doses; showed CV and renal outcomes benefit (CANVAS, CREDENCE trials)
canagliflozin.biosimilar_note: Small molecule; losing market share to empagliflozin and dapagliflozin
canagliflozin.revenue_note: Declining — peak ~$1.5B, significantly lower now as class competition intensified

sitagliptin.mechanism: Dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitor — prevents breakdown of endogenous GLP-1 and GIP, enhancing glucose-dependent insulin release
sitagliptin.biosimilar_note: Patent expired 2022 — generics now widely available; significant revenue erosion underway for Merck
sitagliptin.revenue_note: Rapidly declining post-genericization — peak ~$5.7B (2017); generics now dominant

saxagliptin.mechanism: DPP-4 inhibitor — selective and reversible; SAVOR-TIMI 53 trial showed increased HF hospitalizations, limiting CV label vs SGLT2 inhibitors
saxagliptin.biosimilar_note: Patent expired — generics available; minimal revenue contribution to AstraZeneca now
saxagliptin.revenue_note: Negligible — generic competition has effectively ended branded revenue

sacubitril-valsartan.black_box_summary: Fetal toxicity — discontinue when pregnancy detected; contraindicated in pregnancy due to ARB component (valsartan)
sacubitril-valsartan.mechanism: Fixed-dose combination of neprilysin inhibitor (sacubitril) and ARB (valsartan) — augments natriuretic peptide system while blocking RAAS, reducing cardiac stress and fibrosis in HFrEF
sacubitril-valsartan.biosimilar_note: Small molecule combination — generic risk after patent expiry ~2025; first generic applications filed
sacubitril-valsartan.revenue_note: ~$5B+ annual revenue (2024); key growth driver for Novartis in cardiovascular

inclisiran.mechanism: Small interfering RNA (siRNA) targeting PCSK9 mRNA in hepatocytes — reduces PCSK9 protein synthesis, upregulates LDL receptors, lowers LDL-C; twice-yearly dosing (unique in class)
inclisiran.biosimilar_note: RNA therapeutic — highly complex manufacturing, no established biosimilar pathway for siRNA
inclisiran.revenue_note: ~$500M and growing slowly (2024); uptake slower than expected due to payer restrictions

finerenone.mechanism: Highly selective non-steroidal MRA — reduces inflammation and fibrosis in kidney and heart by blocking aldosterone/cortisol at mineralocorticoid receptors without progesterone/androgen side effects of steroidal MRAs
finerenone.biosimilar_note: Small molecule with long patent runway; no generic competition for many years
finerenone.revenue_note: ~$600M and growing (2024); indicated for CKD with T2D — large addressable market

pembrolizumab.mechanism: Humanized IgG4 anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody — blocks interaction between PD-1 on T-cells and PD-L1/PD-L2 on tumor cells, restoring cytotoxic T-cell activity against tumors
pembrolizumab.biosimilar_note: Biologic — major patent expires 2028; multiple biosimilar programs in development globally; represents the single largest biosimilar commercial opportunity in history
pembrolizumab.revenue_note: ~$25B+ annual revenue (2024) — world's best-selling drug; approved in 40+ tumor types

nivolumab.mechanism: Fully human IgG4 anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody; often combined with ipilimumab (CTLA-4 inhibitor) for dual checkpoint blockade (Opdualag adds LAG-3 inhibition)
nivolumab.biosimilar_note: Biologic — patents expiring 2026-2028; biosimilar applications already filed; significant revenue risk for BMS
nivolumab.revenue_note: ~$9B annual revenue (2024); second-largest IO drug after Keytruda

osimertinib.mechanism: Irreversible, CNS-penetrant EGFR inhibitor — targets T790M resistance mutation and common sensitizing mutations (exon 19 del, L858R); also active in leptomeningeal metastases
osimertinib.biosimilar_note: Small molecule — generic risk post-2031; but LAURA trial adjuvant data extends franchise
osimertinib.revenue_note: ~$7B annual revenue (2024); single largest revenue drug for AstraZeneca

atezolizumab.mechanism: Fc-engineered IgG1 anti-PD-L1 monoclonal antibody — blocks PD-L1 on tumor cells and tumor-infiltrating immune cells; Fc engineering reduces antibody-dependent cytotoxicity against T-cells
atezolizumab.biosimilar_note: Biologic — losing significant market share to pembrolizumab; revenue declining; Roche discontinuing some indications
atezolizumab.revenue_note: ~$1.5B and declining (2024); Roche withdrew from several US indications in 2023-2024

durvalumab.mechanism: Human IgG1 anti-PD-L1 monoclonal antibody; differentiated in unresectable Stage III NSCLC (PACIFIC trial — consolidation after chemoradiation) and biliary tract cancer (TOPAZ-1 trial)
durvalumab.biosimilar_note: Biologic with strong Stage III NSCLC franchise; growing in hepatobiliary indications
durvalumab.revenue_note: ~$3.5B and growing (2024); important second IO franchise for AstraZeneca alongside osimertinib

palbociclib.mechanism: Cyclin-dependent kinase 4 and 6 (CDK4/6) inhibitor — blocks cell cycle progression from G1 to S phase in HR+/HER2- breast cancer; used with aromatase inhibitors or fulvestrant
palbociclib.biosimilar_note: Small molecule — generic competition expected ~2027; first CDK4/6 inhibitor to market (2015)
palbociclib.revenue_note: ~$5B annual revenue (2024); declining as abemaciclib and ribociclib gain adjuvant approvals

ribociclib.mechanism: Selective CDK4/6 inhibitor; MONALEESA trials demonstrated OS benefit in premenopausal women — unique overall survival data differentiating it from palbociclib; used with aromatase inhibitors
ribociclib.biosimilar_note: Small molecule; gaining share due to OS survival data in premenopausal patients
ribociclib.revenue_note: ~$2B and growing (2024); growing faster than palbociclib due to adjuvant data

abemaciclib.mechanism: Continuous-dosing CDK4/6 inhibitor (unlike palbociclib/ribociclib which are intermittent) — more potent CDK4 selectivity; monarchE trial showed significant benefit as adjuvant therapy in high-risk early breast cancer
abemaciclib.biosimilar_note: Small molecule; fastest-growing CDK4/6 inhibitor due to early breast cancer adjuvant approval
abemaciclib.revenue_note: ~$4.5B and growing rapidly (2024); adjuvant indication dramatically expanded addressable market

trastuzumab deruxtecan.black_box_summary: Interstitial lung disease (ILD) and pneumonitis, including fatal cases — monitor patients for signs/symptoms; embryo-fetal toxicity
trastuzumab deruxtecan.company_note: Co-developed and co-commercialized with Daiichi Sankyo; AstraZeneca holds ex-Japan rights
trastuzumab deruxtecan.mechanism: Anti-HER2 antibody (trastuzumab) conjugated to topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (DXd) via stable tetrapeptide linker — delivers cytotoxic payload selectively to HER2-expressing cells; bystander killing effect on adjacent tumor cells
trastuzumab deruxtecan.biosimilar_note: Complex ADC — antibody plus linker plus payload; extremely difficult to replicate; long patent runway
trastuzumab deruxtecan.revenue_note: ~$3.5B and growing very rapidly (2024); expanding across HER2-positive and HER2-low tumor types

olaparib.company_note: Co-developed and co-commercialized with Merck (MSD); AstraZeneca holds manufacturing and lead commercial rights
olaparib.mechanism: Poly (ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) 1/2 inhibitor — traps PARP on damaged DNA, causing replication fork collapse in BRCA1/2-mutated cancer cells that cannot complete homologous recombination repair (synthetic lethality)
olaparib.biosimilar_note: Small molecule; first PARP inhibitor approved; generic risk post-2027
olaparib.revenue_note: ~$2.5B annual revenue (2024); facing competition from rucaparib, niraparib, talazoparib

bevacizumab.black_box_summary: Gastrointestinal perforations, wound healing complications, and hemorrhage — serious and fatal events reported
bevacizumab.mechanism: Humanized IgG1 anti-VEGF-A monoclonal antibody — inhibits VEGF binding to receptors, blocking tumor angiogenesis and vascular permeability
bevacizumab.biosimilar_note: Patent expired 2019 — multiple FDA-approved biosimilars now on market (Mvasi, Zirabev, Alymsys, Vegzelma, Avzivi, Jobevne); Avastin revenue has declined substantially
bevacizumab.revenue_note: Avastin brand declining due to biosimilar competition; Roche total bevacizumab revenue (brand + royalties) still ~$1B

cetuximab.black_box_summary: Cardiopulmonary arrest and sudden death in patients with squamous cell carcinoma of head and neck receiving radiation; severe infusion reactions — discontinue for serious reactions
cetuximab.company_note: Eli Lilly holds US and Canada commercial rights; Merck KGaA holds European rights (different company from Merck/MSD)
cetuximab.mechanism: Chimeric IgG1 anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody — blocks EGFR ligand binding, inhibiting downstream RAS/RAF/MAPK and PI3K/AKT signaling; only active in RAS wild-type colorectal cancer
cetuximab.biosimilar_note: Patent expired — biosimilar development ongoing; older drug losing relevance vs newer targeted therapies
cetuximab.revenue_note: Declining — ~$500M globally; generics and newer agents reducing use

lecanemab.black_box_summary: Amyloid-related imaging abnormalities (ARIA) — including ARIA with edema/effusion (ARIA-E) and ARIA with microhemorrhages (ARIA-H); serious and life-threatening events including death reported; risk higher in APOE e4 homozygotes
lecanemab.company_note: Co-developed and co-commercialized with Biogen; Eisai leads global development and commercialization
lecanemab.mechanism: Humanized IgG1 anti-amyloid beta protofibril antibody — selectively binds soluble amyloid beta aggregates (protofibrils) and insoluble plaques, promoting clearance; CLARITY AD trial showed 27% slowing of cognitive decline
lecanemab.biosimilar_note: Novel biologic with long patent runway; first disease-modifying Alzheimer's therapy with full FDA approval
lecanemab.revenue_note: ~$200M (2024) — very slow uptake due to ARIA safety monitoring requirements, limited infusion center capacity, and payer access hurdles

donanemab.black_box_summary: Amyloid-related imaging abnormalities (ARIA) — ARIA-E and ARIA-H including serious and life-threatening events; three deaths in TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 trial; higher risk in APOE e4 homozygotes
donanemab.mechanism: IgG1 anti-N3pG amyloid beta monoclonal antibody — targets a modified/pyroglutamate form of amyloid that appears early in plaque formation; TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 showed 35% slowing of decline and unique treatment-until-clearance dosing design
donanemab.biosimilar_note: Very new biologic; long patent runway; competes directly with lecanemab in early Alzheimer's
donanemab.revenue_note: Approved July 2024 — very early commercialization; competing with lecanemab for a limited but growing patient population
